message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding process_priority parameter to click.edit()/Editor class for CPU priority control of editor subprocess

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding multi-file support to click.edit(): edit_file->edit_files, filename accepts str or Iterable[str]

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and click.edit(); added Editor._apply_process_priority(pid) helper; edit_file() now calls self._apply_process_priority(c.pid) after Popen. If you rename edit_file->edit_files, please keep the _apply_process_priority call after each Popen so both features compose.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Renamed edit_file->edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in Editor class; single Popen call now joins all filenames into one quoted command string. termui.edit() normalizes filename (str or Iterable[str]) into a tuple and calls ed.edit_files(filenames). Please add your _apply_process_priority(c.pid) call right after the single subprocess.Popen(...) inside edit_files - there's only one Popen call now, not one per file, since all filenames are passed to a single editor invocation.
